text.title: Docker Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns commonly observed when applying the docker methodology. Each pairs a symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Container compromise grants root-equivalent capability on the host namespace.
antipattern.root-cause: Image has no `USER` directive or runs as `USER root`.
antipattern.fix: Add `USER nonroot` (or numeric UID) in final stage; verify with `docker inspect`.

antipattern.symptom: Builds are non-reproducible; upstream image swaps inject untracked changes.
antipattern.root-cause: Base image referenced by `:latest` or named tag without digest.
antipattern.fix: Pin to digest: `FROM image@sha256:...`; automate digest refresh via Renovate/Dependabot.

antipattern.symptom: Secrets and large directories leak into build context; image bloats.
antipattern.fix: Add `.dockerignore` covering `.git`, secrets, `node_modules`, build artefacts; rebuild + measure context size.

antipattern.symptom: Image carries compiler + source + dev deps; widens attack surface.
antipattern.root-cause: Final image >500 MB for a binary that fits in <50 MB.
antipattern.fix: Switch to multi-stage build; final stage = distroless + binary only.

antipattern.root-cause: CI pipeline lacks Artifact Analysis / Trivy step before push.
antipattern.fix: Add scan step; fail build on CRITICAL CVEs; document exception process.
